subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **21 item cards** in 7 types (Geisha items): 2, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4, 5 cards per type
- **7 Geisha cards** (victory markers with charm values 2-5)
- **8 action tokens** (4 per player)
- **7 victory markers**

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place 7 Geisha cards** in a row between players.
2. **Place victory markers** on each Geisha card (neutral position).
3. **Shuffle 21 item cards** — Remove 1 secretly. Deal 6 to each player.
4. **Remaining 8 cards** form the draw pile.
5. **Each player** takes 4 action tokens.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **6 cards in hand.**
- **4 action tokens** (face up): Secret, Trade, Gift, Competition.
- **Each action used exactly once** per round.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Hanamikoji is a tense 2-player card game of dilemmas. Win the favor of Geisha by playing item cards, but the 4 actions you take each create painful choices about what to give to your opponent.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Players alternate turns. On your turn:
1. **Draw 1 card** from the draw pile (adding to your hand).
2. **Perform 1 action** — Use one of your 4 unused action tokens.

Each action token can only be used once per round. After 4 turns each (8 total), the round ends.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### The 4 Actions
- **Secret** — Place 1 card face down. It's added to your side at round end (opponent doesn't see it).
- **Trade** — Place 2 cards face down. They are removed from the game (neither player gets them).
- **Gift** — Place 3 cards face up. Your opponent chooses 1; you get the other 2.
- **Competition** — Place 4 cards face up in 2 pairs. Your opponent chooses 1 pair; you get the other.

#### Claiming Geisha
- Place item cards on your side of the corresponding Geisha.
- The player with more item cards of a Geisha's type wins her favor (moves marker to their side).
- Ties leave the marker in its current position.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **1 removed card** — One item card is removed at start, creating uncertainty.
- **Action order** — You choose when to use each action, creating strategic timing.
- **Persistent favor** — Geisha favor carries over between rounds unless one player gains majority.
- **All 4 actions mandatory** — You must use all 4 actions each round.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Win Conditions (checked each round end)
- **4 Geisha** — Win the favor of 4 or more Geisha.
- **11 Charm** — Win Geisha totaling 11+ charm points.

#### Multiple Rounds
- If neither player wins, play another round. Geisha favor persists.
- The game usually ends in 1-3 rounds.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Gift wisely** — In the Gift action, give away your weakest cards.
- **Secret your best** — Hide a key card that clinches an important Geisha.
- **Trade away waste** — Remove cards you don't need and your opponent might.
- **Watch opponent's actions** — Their remaining actions constrain their options.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Action timing** — Use Competition early when you have more cards to absorb the loss.
- **5-charm Geisha** — The 5 card Geisha is hardest to win but worth the most.
- **Gift poisoning** — Offer 3 cards that are all useful to you but force a painful choice.
- **Information control** — Use Secret early before your opponent knows your strategy.
